33: module apb_gpio
34: #(

37: (

42: 
43:     input  logic [APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] PADDR,
44:     input  logic               [31:0] PWDATA,
45:     input  logic                      PWRITE,
46:     input  logic                      PSEL,
47:     input  logic                      PENABLE,
48:     output logic               [31:0] PRDATA,
49:     output logic                      PREADY,
50:     output logic                      PSLVERR,
51: 
52:     input  logic               [31:0] gpio_in,
53:     output logic               [31:0] gpio_in_sync,
54:     output logic               [31:0] gpio_out,
55:     output logic               [31:0] gpio_dir,
56:     output logic          [31:0][5:0] gpio_padcfg,
57:     output logic                      interrupt

59: 
60:     logic [31:0] r_gpio_inten;
61:     logic [31:0] r_gpio_inttype0;
62:     logic [31:0] s_gpio_inttype0;
63:     logic [31:0] r_gpio_inttype1;
64:     logic [31:0] s_gpio_inttype1;
65:     logic [31:0] r_gpio_out;
66:     logic [31:0] r_gpio_dir;
67:     logic [31:0] r_gpio_sync0;
68:     logic [31:0] r_gpio_sync1;
69:     logic [31:0] r_gpio_in;
70:     logic [31:0] r_gpio_en;
71:     logic [31:0] r_gpio_lock;
72:     logic [31:0] s_gpio_rise;
73:     logic [31:0] s_gpio_fall;
74:     logic [31:0] s_is_int_rise;
75:     logic [31:0] s_is_int_rifa;
76:     logic [31:0] s_is_int_fall;
77:     logic [31:0] s_is_int_all;
78:     logic        s_rise_int;
79: 
80:     logic  [4:0] s_apb_addr;
81: 
82:     logic [31:0] r_status;
83: 
84:     logic [7:0] s_clk_en;
85:     logic [7:0] s_clkg;

87:     genvar i;
88: 
89:     assign s_apb_addr = PADDR[6:2];
90: 
91:     assign gpio_in_sync = r_gpio_sync1;
92: 
93:     assign s_gpio_rise =  r_gpio_sync1 & ~r_gpio_in; //foreach input check if rising edge
94:     assign s_gpio_fall = ~r_gpio_sync1 &  r_gpio_in; //foreach input check if falling edge
95: 
96:     assign s_is_int_fall =  ~s_gpio_inttype1 & ~s_gpio_inttype0 & s_gpio_fall;                 // inttype 00 fall
97:     assign s_is_int_rise =  ~s_gpio_inttype1 &  s_gpio_inttype0 & s_gpio_rise;                 // inttype 01 rise
98:     assign s_is_int_rifa =   s_gpio_inttype1 & ~s_gpio_inttype0 & (s_gpio_rise | s_gpio_fall); // inttype 10 rise
99: 
100:     //check if bit if interrupt is enable and if interrupt specified by inttype occurred
101:     assign s_is_int_all  = r_gpio_inten & r_gpio_en & (s_is_int_rise | s_is_int_fall | s_is_int_rifa);
102: 
103:     //is any bit enabled and specified interrupt happened?
104:     assign s_rise_int = |s_is_int_all;
105: 
106:     assign interrupt = s_rise_int;
107: 
108:     always_comb begin
109:         for (int i=0;i<16;i++)
110:         begin
111:             s_gpio_inttype0[i]    = r_gpio_inttype0[i*2];
112:             s_gpio_inttype0[16+i] = r_gpio_inttype1[i*2];
113:             s_gpio_inttype1[i]    = r_gpio_inttype0[i*2+1];
114:             s_gpio_inttype1[16+i] = r_gpio_inttype1[i*2+1];
115:         end
116:     end

118:     always_ff @(posedge HCLK, negedge HRESETn)

120:         if(~HRESETn)
121:         begin
122:             r_status  <=  'h0;
123:         end
124:         else
125:         begin
126:             if (s_rise_int ) //rise interrupt if not already rise
127:             begin
128:                 r_status  <= r_status | s_is_int_all;
129:             end
130:             else if (PSEL && PENABLE && !PWRITE && (s_apb_addr == `REG_INTSTATUS)) //clears int if status is read
131:             begin
132:                r_status  <=  'h0;
133:             end
134:         end
135:     end

137:     generate
138:         for(i=0;i<8;i++)
139:             pulp_clock_gating i_clk_gate

141:                 .clk_i(HCLK),

146:     endgenerate

148:     always_comb begin : proc_clk_en
149:         for (int i=0;i<8;i++)

154:     always_ff @(posedge s_clkg[0], negedge HRESETn)
155:     begin
156:         if(~HRESETn)
157:         begin
158:             r_gpio_sync0[3:0]    <= 'h0;
159:             r_gpio_sync1[3:0]    <= 'h0;
160:             r_gpio_in[3:0]       <= 'h0;
161:         end
162:         else
163:         begin
164:             r_gpio_sync0[3:0]    <= gpio_in[3:0];      //first 2 sync for metastability resolving
165:             r_gpio_sync1[3:0]    <= r_gpio_sync0[3:0];
166:             r_gpio_in[3:0]       <= r_gpio_sync1[3:0]; //last reg used for edge detection
167:         end
168:     end //always
169: 
170:     always_ff @(posedge s_clkg[1], negedge HRESETn)
171:     begin
172:         if(~HRESETn)
173:         begin
174:             r_gpio_sync0[7:4]    <= 'h0;
175:             r_gpio_sync1[7:4]    <= 'h0;
176:             r_gpio_in[7:4]       <= 'h0;
177:         end
178:         else
179:         begin
180:             r_gpio_sync0[7:4]    <= gpio_in[7:4];      //first 2 sync for metastability resolving
181:             r_gpio_sync1[7:4]    <= r_gpio_sync0[7:4];
182:             r_gpio_in[7:4]       <= r_gpio_sync1[7:4]; //last reg used for edge detection
183:         end
184:     end //always
185: 
186:     always_ff @(posedge s_clkg[2], negedge HRESETn)
187:     begin
188:         if(~HRESETn)
189:         begin
190:             r_gpio_sync0[11:8]    <= 'h0;
191:             r_gpio_sync1[11:8]    <= 'h0;
192:             r_gpio_in[11:8]       <= 'h0;
193:         end
194:         else
195:         begin
196:             r_gpio_sync0[11:8]    <= gpio_in[11:8];      //first 2 sync for metastability resolving
197:             r_gpio_sync1[11:8]    <= r_gpio_sync0[11:8];
198:             r_gpio_in[11:8]       <= r_gpio_sync1[11:8]; //last reg used for edge detection
199:         end
200:     end //always
201: 
202:     always_ff @(posedge s_clkg[3], negedge HRESETn)
203:     begin
204:         if(~HRESETn)
205:         begin
206:             r_gpio_sync0[15:12]    <= 'h0;
207:             r_gpio_sync1[15:12]    <= 'h0;
208:             r_gpio_in[15:12]       <= 'h0;
209:         end
210:         else
211:         begin
212:             r_gpio_sync0[15:12]    <= gpio_in[15:12];      //first 2 sync for metastability resolving
213:             r_gpio_sync1[15:12]    <= r_gpio_sync0[15:12];
214:             r_gpio_in[15:12]       <= r_gpio_sync1[15:12]; //last reg used for edge detection
215:         end
216:     end //always

219:     begin
220:         if(~HRESETn)
221:         begin
222:             r_gpio_sync0[19:16]    <= 'h0;
223:             r_gpio_sync1[19:16]    <= 'h0;
224:             r_gpio_in[19:16]       <= 'h0;
225:         end
226:         else
227:         begin

95: 
96:     assign s_is_int_fall =  ~s_gpio_inttype1 & ~s_gpio_inttype0 & s_gpio_fall;                 // inttype 00 fall
97:     assign s_is_int_rise =  ~s_gpio_inttype1 &  s_gpio_inttype0 & s_gpio_rise;                 // inttype 01 rise
98:     assign s_is_int_rifa =   s_gpio_inttype1 & ~s_gpio_inttype0 & (s_gpio_rise | s_gpio_fall); // inttype 10 rise
99: 
100:     //check if bit if interrupt is enable and if interrupt specified by inttype occurred
101:     assign s_is_int_all  = r_gpio_inten & r_gpio_en & (s_is_int_rise | s_is_int_fall | s_is_int_rifa);

33: module apb_gpio
34: #(
35:     parameter APB_ADDR_WIDTH = 12  //APB slaves are 4KB by default
36: )
37: (
38:     input  logic                      HCLK,
39:     input  logic                      HRESETn,

42: 
43:     input  logic [APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] PADDR,
44:     input  logic               [31:0] PWDATA,
45:     input  logic                      PWRITE,
46:     input  logic                      PSEL,
47:     input  logic                      PENABLE,
48:     output logic               [31:0] PRDATA,
49:     output logic                      PREADY,
50:     output logic                      PSLVERR,
51: 
52:     input  logic               [31:0] gpio_in,
53:     output logic               [31:0] gpio_in_sync,
54:     output logic               [31:0] gpio_out,
55:     output logic               [31:0] gpio_dir,
56:     output logic          [31:0][5:0] gpio_padcfg,
57:     output logic                      interrupt
58: );

60:     logic [31:0] r_gpio_inten;
61:     logic [31:0] r_gpio_inttype0;
62:     logic [31:0] s_gpio_inttype0;
63:     logic [31:0] r_gpio_inttype1;
64:     logic [31:0] s_gpio_inttype1;
65:     logic [31:0] r_gpio_out;
66:     logic [31:0] r_gpio_dir;
67:     logic [31:0] r_gpio_sync0;
68:     logic [31:0] r_gpio_sync1;
69:     logic [31:0] r_gpio_in;
70:     logic [31:0] r_gpio_en;
71:     logic [31:0] r_gpio_lock;
72:     logic [31:0] s_gpio_rise;
73:     logic [31:0] s_gpio_fall;
74:     logic [31:0] s_is_int_rise;
75:     logic [31:0] s_is_int_rifa;
76:     logic [31:0] s_is_int_fall;
77:     logic [31:0] s_is_int_all;
78:     logic        s_rise_int;
79: 
80:     logic  [4:0] s_apb_addr;
81: 
82:     logic [31:0] r_status;
83: 
84:     logic [7:0] s_clk_en;
85:     logic [7:0] s_clkg;
86: 
87:     genvar i;
88: 
89:     assign s_apb_addr = PADDR[6:2];
90: 
91:     assign gpio_in_sync = r_gpio_sync1;
92: 
93:     assign s_gpio_rise =  r_gpio_sync1 & ~r_gpio_in; //foreach input check if rising edge
94:     assign s_gpio_fall = ~r_gpio_sync1 &  r_gpio_in; //foreach input check if falling edge
95: 
96:     assign s_is_int_fall =  ~s_gpio_inttype1 & ~s_gpio_inttype0 & s_gpio_fall;                 // inttype 00 fall
97:     assign s_is_int_rise =  ~s_gpio_inttype1 &  s_gpio_inttype0 & s_gpio_rise;                 // inttype 01 rise
98:     assign s_is_int_rifa =   s_gpio_inttype1 & ~s_gpio_inttype0 & (s_gpio_rise | s_gpio_fall); // inttype 10 rise
99: 
100:     //check if bit if interrupt is enable and if interrupt specified by inttype occurred
101:     assign s_is_int_all  = r_gpio_inten & r_gpio_en & (s_is_int_rise | s_is_int_fall | s_is_int_rifa);
102: 
103:     //is any bit enabled and specified interrupt happened?
104:     assign s_rise_int = |s_is_int_all;
105: 
106:     assign interrupt = s_rise_int;
107: 
108:     always_comb begin
109:         for (int i=0;i<16;i++)
110:         begin
111:             s_gpio_inttype0[i]    = r_gpio_inttype0[i*2];
112:             s_gpio_inttype0[16+i] = r_gpio_inttype1[i*2];
113:             s_gpio_inttype1[i]    = r_gpio_inttype0[i*2+1];
114:             s_gpio_inttype1[16+i] = r_gpio_inttype1[i*2+1];
115:         end
116:     end
117: 
118:     always_ff @(posedge HCLK, negedge HRESETn)
119:     begin
120:         if(~HRESETn)
121:         begin
122:             r_status  <=  'h0;
123:         end
124:         else
125:         begin
126:             if (s_rise_int ) //rise interrupt if not already rise
127:             begin
128:                 r_status  <= r_status | s_is_int_all;
129:             end
130:             else if (PSEL && PENABLE && !PWRITE && (s_apb_addr == `REG_INTSTATUS)) //clears int if status is read
131:             begin
132:                r_status  <=  'h0;
133:             end
134:         end
135:     end
136: 
137:     generate
138:         for(i=0;i<8;i++)
139:             pulp_clock_gating i_clk_gate
140:             (
141:                 .clk_i(HCLK),
142:                 .en_i(s_clk_en[i]),
143:                 .test_en_i(dft_cg_enable_i),
144:                 .clk_o(s_clkg[i])
145:             );
146:     endgenerate
147: 
148:     always_comb begin : proc_clk_en
149:         for (int i=0;i<8;i++)
150:             s_clk_en[i] = r_gpio_en[i*4] | r_gpio_en[i*4+1] | r_gpio_en[i*4+2] | r_gpio_en[i*4+3];
151:     end
152: 
153: 
154:     always_ff @(posedge s_clkg[0], negedge HRESETn)
155:     begin
156:         if(~HRESETn)
157:         begin
158:             r_gpio_sync0[3:0]    <= 'h0;
159:             r_gpio_sync1[3:0]    <= 'h0;
160:             r_gpio_in[3:0]       <= 'h0;
161:         end
162:         else
163:         begin
164:             r_gpio_sync0[3:0]    <= gpio_in[3:0];      //first 2 sync for metastability resolving
165:             r_gpio_sync1[3:0]    <= r_gpio_sync0[3:0];
166:             r_gpio_in[3:0]       <= r_gpio_sync1[3:0]; //last reg used for edge detection
167:         end
168:     end //always
169: 
170:     always_ff @(posedge s_clkg[1], negedge HRESETn)
171:     begin
172:         if(~HRESETn)
173:         begin
174:             r_gpio_sync0[7:4]    <= 'h0;
175:             r_gpio_sync1[7:4]    <= 'h0;
176:             r_gpio_in[7:4]       <= 'h0;
177:         end
178:         else
179:         begin
180:             r_gpio_sync0[7:4]    <= gpio_in[7:4];      //first 2 sync for metastability resolving
181:             r_gpio_sync1[7:4]    <= r_gpio_sync0[7:4];
182:             r_gpio_in[7:4]       <= r_gpio_sync1[7:4]; //last reg used for edge detection
183:         end
184:     end //always
185: 
186:     always_ff @(posedge s_clkg[2], negedge HRESETn)
187:     begin
188:         if(~HRESETn)
189:         begin
190:             r_gpio_sync0[11:8]    <= 'h0;
191:             r_gpio_sync1[11:8]    <= 'h0;
192:             r_gpio_in[11:8]       <= 'h0;
193:         end
194:         else
195:         begin
196:             r_gpio_sync0[11:8]    <= gpio_in[11:8];      //first 2 sync for metastability resolving
197:             r_gpio_sync1[11:8]    <= r_gpio_sync0[11:8];
198:             r_gpio_in[11:8]       <= r_gpio_sync1[11:8]; //last reg used for edge detection
199:         end
200:     end //always
201: 
202:     always_ff @(posedge s_clkg[3], negedge HRESETn)
203:     begin
204:         if(~HRESETn)
205:         begin
206:             r_gpio_sync0[15:12]    <= 'h0;
207:             r_gpio_sync1[15:12]    <= 'h0;
208:             r_gpio_in[15:12]       <= 'h0;
209:         end
210:         else
211:         begin
212:             r_gpio_sync0[15:12]    <= gpio_in[15:12];      //first 2 sync for metastability resolving
213:             r_gpio_sync1[15:12]    <= r_gpio_sync0[15:12];
214:             r_gpio_in[15:12]       <= r_gpio_sync1[15:12]; //last reg used for edge detection
215:         end
216:     end //always
217: 
218:     always_ff @(posedge s_clkg[4], negedge HRESETn)
219:     begin
220:         if(~HRESETn)
221:         begin
222:             r_gpio_sync0[19:16]    <= 'h0;
223:             r_gpio_sync1[19:16]    <= 'h0;
224:             r_gpio_in[19:16]       <= 'h0;
225:         end
226:         else
227:         begin
228:             r_gpio_sync0[19:16]    <= gpio_in[19:16];      //first 2 sync for metastability resolving
229:             r_gpio_sync1[19:16]    <= r_gpio_sync0[19:16];
230:             r_gpio_in[19:16]       <= r_gpio_sync1[19:16]; //last reg used for edge detection
231:         end
